extractions.problem: Inferring cell-cell communication from single-cell transcriptomics data by predicting enriched cellular interactions between different cell types.
extractions.method: Statistical framework integrated with a database of ligands, receptors, and their interactions, accounting for subunit architecture of multi-subunit complexes.
extractions.finding: CellPhoneDB enables evaluation of meaningful biological interactions from scRNA-seq data in ~2 hours for typical datasets.
extractions.dataset: Published single-cell transcriptomics datasets (example: 10,000 cells, 19 cell types)
extractions.baseline: Other ligand-receptor repositories without subunit architecture consideration
extractions.result: ~2h runtime for 10GB dataset with 10,000 cells and 19 cell types

extractions.problem: Reveal cellular diversity in mouse brain cortex and hippocampus beyond morphological classification.
extractions.method: Single-cell RNA-seq transcriptome analysis and clustering of brain cells.
extractions.finding: Identified multiple molecular subtypes within seemingly homogeneous cell classes; interneurons of similar type span dissimilar brain regions; oligodendrocytes classified into ~6 molecular subtypes.
extractions.metric: RNA expression clustering; molecular subtype identification
extractions.result: >6 oligodendrocyte subtypes; microglia distinguished from perivascular macrophages

extractions.problem: Efficient calculation of molecular polar surface area for predicting drug transport properties in high-throughput screening.
extractions.method: Topological PSA (TPSA) based on summation of tabulated surface contributions of polar fragments.
extractions.finding: TPSA provides results practically identical to 3D PSA calculation with dramatically reduced computational time.
extractions.metric: Correlation coefficient between 3D PSA and TPSA; computation speed

extractions.problem: Design efficient drug-like chemical libraries by identifying physicochemical property ranges of known drugs.
extractions.method: Quantitative characterization of drug databases by computed properties (log P, molar refractivity, MW, atom count) and qualitative analysis of functional groups and substructures.
extractions.finding: Identified property ranges covering >80% of known drugs in multiple therapeutic categories.
extractions.dataset: Comprehensive Medicinal Chemistry database and subsets (CNS, cardiovascular, cancer, inflammation, infection)
extractions.result: log P: -0.4 to 5.6 (avg 2.52); MW: 160-480 (avg 357); molar refractivity: 40-130 (avg 97); atoms: 20-70 (avg 48)

extractions.problem: Correcting batch effects in scRNA-seq data from different laboratories and times that compromise data integration.
extractions.method: Mutual nearest neighbors (MNN) detection in high-dimensional expression space without assuming equal or known cell population composition across batches.
extractions.finding: MNN correction method outperforms existing methods and scales to large numbers of cells without batch effect bias.
extractions.metric: Batch effect correction accuracy; scalability to cell number
extractions.baseline: Existing batch correction methods (assuming equal/known population composition)
extractions.result: Superior performance vs existing methods; scales to large datasets

extractions.problem: Comprehensive assessment of ADMET properties for drugs, pesticides, chemicals, and other compounds.
extractions.method: ADMET structure-activity relationship database with 22 qualitative classification models and 5 quantitative regression models trained on curated literature data.
extractions.finding: Provides predictive models with high accuracy for estimating ADMET properties of novel chemicals.
extractions.dataset: >96,000 unique compounds with >210,000 ADMET annotated data points; 45 ADMET-associated properties
extractions.result: 96,000+ compounds; 45 ADMET properties; high-accuracy prediction models

extractions.problem: Quantify absolute metabolite concentrations in E. coli to enable quantitative understanding of cellular metabolism.
extractions.method: Liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) to measure >100 metabolite concentrations in aerobic exponentially growing cells.
extractions.finding: Total intracellular metabolite pool ~300 mM with glutamate most abundant; metabolite concentrations exceed Km for most substrate-enzyme pairs except in lower glycolysis where reactions are near equilibrium.
extractions.dataset: E. coli (aerobic, exponential growth; glucose, glycerol, or acetate as carbon source)
extractions.result: ~300 mM total pool; lower glycolysis intermediates near equilibrium

extractions.problem: Understand continuous developmental progression in B cell lymphopoiesis as a continuum of transitional states rather than discrete cell subsets.
extractions.method: Single-cell mass cytometry combined with Wanderlust algorithm to align cells onto unified developmental trajectory.
extractions.finding: Trajectory reveals regulatory checkpoints where signals are rewired (IL-7/STAT5 pathway) paralleling cellular developmental state changes and immunoglobulin rearrangement events.
extractions.dataset: Human hematopoietic stem cells through naive B cells
extractions.metric: Trajectory alignment quality; regulatory signal correlation with developmental stage
extractions.result: Complete trajectory from hematopoietic stem cells to naive B cells with identified regulatory checkpoints

extractions.problem: Understand how bacterial cell composition (RNA, protein) and gene expression change with exponential growth rate.
extractions.method: Review and synthesis of studies on chemical composition of E. coli and S. enterica cells at different exponential growth rates.
extractions.finding: RNA and protein amounts linked to growth rate through ribosome function; gene expression spectrum varies with growth medium despite same growth rate due to ribosome binding site availability.
extractions.dataset: E. coli and S. enterica at different exponential growth rates
extractions.metric: Growth rate (doubling time); RNA and protein content; C-period, D-period, ribosome activity
extractions.result: Equations describing macromolecular composition as function of doubling time and five parameters

extractions.problem: Understand how finite cellular resources create trade-offs that affect protein synthesis, gene expression, and growth rate.
extractions.method: Mathematical cell model incorporating protein synthesis mechanisms and resource constraints that respect cellular trade-offs.
extractions.finding: Model quantitatively recovers typical growth behavior of individual cells and cell populations despite resource constraints.
extractions.dataset: Single growing cells; bacterial and eukaryotic cell populations
extractions.metric: Growth rate; protein synthesis rate; resource allocation efficiency
extractions.baseline: Models not accounting for cellular trade-offs
extractions.result: Quantitative recovery of observed cell growth behavior with resource constraints

extractions.problem: Explain presence of memory-phenotype CD4+ T cells specific to viral antigens in adults who were never infected.
extractions.method: Flow cytometry analysis of CD4+ T cell populations; TCR specificity analysis and cross-reactivity studies with environmental antigens.
extractions.finding: 20-90% (average >50%) of CD4+ T cells show memory phenotype to viral antigens in unexposed adults; mechanism is TCR cross-reactivity to environmental antigens; contrast with newborn naive repertoire.
extractions.dataset: Adult blood samples; newborn blood; HIV-1 and influenza-reactive T cell populations
extractions.metric: Percentage of memory-phenotype cells; TCR cross-reactivity frequency
extractions.baseline: Newborn T cell repertoire (predominantly naive phenotype)
extractions.result: 20-90% memory phenotype in unexposed adults (avg >50%) vs naive in newborns

extractions.problem: Characterize quantitative relationships between bacterial cell composition and growth rate; understand coupling of gene expression to growth state.
extractions.method: Review of bacterial growth laws and phenomenological approaches exploiting quantitative empirical relationships.
extractions.finding: Growth laws reveal underlying design principles that enable predictive manipulation of cell behavior without detailed molecular knowledge.
extractions.dataset: Bacterial cells across multiple species and growth conditions
extractions.metric: Growth rate; cell composition ratios (RNA/protein); gene expression level
extractions.result: Quantitative empirical growth laws identified; design principles revealed

extractions.problem: Understand the context-dependent and evolvable nature of gene essentiality across organisms.
extractions.method: Review of gene essentiality screens in bacteria, yeasts, animals, and human cells.
extractions.finding: Gene essentiality is not a static binary property but context-dependent and evolvable; has important implications for cancer and infectious disease treatment strategies.
extractions.dataset: Genes from bacteria, yeasts, animals, human cells
extractions.metric: Essential vs non-essential classification; context dependency
extractions.result: Context-dependent, non-absolute nature of gene essentiality across kingdoms

extractions.problem: Enable flexible and reconfigurable robotic assembly systems to adapt to product personalization and hybrid human-robot workflows.
extractions.method: Digital twin infrastructure with virtual shopfloor representation (CAD+sensor fusion), semantic data modeling, and real-time synchronization via ROS framework.
extractions.finding: Digital twin successfully applied to automotive assembly; infrastructure designed for generic multi-case deployment.
extractions.dataset: Automotive assembly case study (car front axle assembly)
extractions.baseline: Fixed robotic assembly systems without digital representation
extractions.result: Functional digital twin for adaptive robotic assembly in automotive production

extractions.problem: Enable computational modeling and simulation of biochemical networks for systems biology analysis without requiring programming expertise.
extractions.method: COPASI software package providing integrated tools for steady-state simulation, time-course simulation, parameter scanning, sensitivity analysis, optimization, parameter estimation, and stochastic simulation.
extractions.finding: COPASI provides convenient access to multiple analyses through user interface without requiring deep knowledge of numerical algorithms.
extractions.dataset: Published biochemical models from BioModels database in SBML format
extractions.metric: Model simulation accuracy; convergence; parameter sensitivity
extractions.result: Integrated software with multiple analysis capabilities for biochemical networks

extractions.problem: Understand iterative development of computational models of cardiac ventricular cell action potentials through cycles of model refinement and experimental validation.
extractions.method: Review of cardiac ventricular cell model development history from 1960 onward, analyzing model failures and successes to guide improvements.
extractions.finding: Learning from model failures is as important as successes; iterative cycles reveal design principles and point toward next-generation models.
extractions.dataset: Cardiac ventricular cells; experimental action potential recordings
extractions.metric: Model-experiment agreement; action potential waveform accuracy
extractions.result: Iterative improvement trajectory from 1960 to present; demonstrated value of failure analysis

extractions.problem: Create a physically and biochemically consistent atomistic model of bacterial cytoplasm integrating structural biology and systems-level metabolism.
extractions.method: Reconstruction of metabolic network from gene annotations; assembly of complete atomistic model with predicted protein structures, RNA, complexes, metabolites, ions, and solvent.
extractions.finding: Model successfully bridges atomistic and cellular scales, connects physical and biochemical aspects, and unifies structural and systems views.
extractions.metric: Model completeness; structure prediction accuracy; metabolic network coverage
extractions.baseline: Prior partial/separate structural and metabolic models
extractions.result: Complete atomistic cytoplasm model of M. genitalium for multi-scale simulation studies

extractions.problem: Predict temporal stochastic fluctuations in signaling molecules in small cellular compartments and test threshold-crossing models of signal detection.
extractions.method: Stochastic simulation of CheYp molecule fluctuations in bacterial chemotactic pathway using molecular-level interaction probabilities derived from experimental concentrations.
extractions.finding: Stochastic fluctuation amplitude and duration increase with decreasing volume; at 10% bacterial volume, average fluctuation duration (80.7 ms) is much shorter than observed motor switching behavior (~2.6 s), inconsistent with simple threshold model.
extractions.dataset: E. coli chemotactic signaling (flagellar motor control)
extractions.metric: Fluctuation amplitude; temporal duration; frequency distribution
extractions.result: 80.7 ms average fluctuation at 10% bacterial volume; incompatible with simple threshold model
